reviews.review_msg: Simon is kind, and he puts an active effort into making class engaging. He does not just read off the slides which is appreciated. The TA's however mark inconsistently. Simon is an amazing prof.

I initially had little interest in this class, but Simon opened my eyes to the world of entrepreneurship in a way I never thought possible. He's an amazing professor, with excellent lectures and experiences, and he's very accessable outside of class to give quality advice. The class assignments were tedious, but easy and fun. Take 240 with Simon!
Simon is by far one of the best professors at SFU, he is extremely polite, his lectures are amazing, and he is such a caring and kind person, I 100% recommend taking BUS 240 with him!

Simon is very dedicated to making the lectures activity-based for bus240, so don't expect to just sit and zone out the entire time. It's clear that he's passionate about his students' involvement too. The assignments can be a bit much if you procrastinate (group projects aswell) but they're extremely manageable if you set aside some time for them.

Simon was a great prof overall. Class attendance was mandatory and took attendance by having a seating chart. There was no final exam, but we did have a lot of assignments that were extremely easy to complete. There were also two group projects which were also straightforward and easy as long as you got a good group.

Simon is easily my favourite professor at SFU. He is very well-spoken (with a good sense of humour), and he actually cares about making his lectures properly organized, presented, and engaging. He expresses a genuine desire to teach and is very approachable, providing detailed and critical feedback. I highly recommend taking BUS240 with him.

Simon is hands down the best prof I've had at SFU. He is extremely well spoken and consistently makes lectures engaging and interactive. If you're looking for a business elective which rewards active participation and engagement, I highly recommend taking BUS 238 with him!

The best professor I've had at SFU. Lectures were engaging and inspiring, and I learned so much from both Simon and the guest speakers he brought in. Simon knew everyone's name and encouraged every student to branch out and make connections with classmates. I enjoyed the projects we had and I'd 100% recommend any course with him.

An absolutely amazing professor. I can confidently say Dr. Ford has been the best prof I've had at SFU. The case competition was extremely fun, and I thoroughly enjoyed my overall experience in the course because of Dr. Ford.

Dr. Ford is the best prof at SFU. He is an amazing instructor, kind, helpful and will always want the best for each of his students. Even if you are not a business student, take this class!! It was a great way to learn about skills that can be used in every major. Excellent experience and will always wish him the best
Great instructor and lots of feedback if you ask for it. Class is very heavy on group projects so be careful of those not getting their work done. Would take as many classes as you can from him.

simon is super energetic and made my first ever business course super exciting! I highly recommend taking a course with him he is great. you will have weekly homework assignments and readings for tutorial but nothing too crazy.

Really enjoyed taking Bus 238 with Professor Ford. I would highly recommend taking a class with him if you have the opportunity. Workload was reasonable for a 200 level class with lots of group work involved. He makes sure you get feedback from your teammates which is really good if you want to improve in the future. His lectures are really fun!

One of the best profs I have ever had. Simon is so inspiring and is passionate about everything he teaches in his lectures. I did not expect the class to be as difficult as it was, it definitely required a ton of work but it was also very rewarding and practical. Taking a class with Simon is amazing but not easy!!

Simon is awesome! He genuinely cares about his students and his work and wants to help whenever possible! The lectures could have been shorter but I enjoyed all the activities he organized! He also is very engaging and makes sure to give positive feedback to everyone!

Simon's one of the best profs at SFU. His personality is really great and makes you want to be in class. But the material in 201 is pretty boring and it's all about memorizing. His teaching style is super concise and he is an entertaining professor to say the least. I really enjoyed his lectures, but you don't really have to show up to class.

He is a nice person and very approachable. Unfortunately, he is not a great prof. He's absolutely terrible! Gives vague instructions for his assignments. He is also super picky. He takes marks off even when you follow the instructions. His lectures are super boring as he recites the textbook. You are going to fall asleep in his class.

Very enthusiastic, empathetic, and kind. Fun to listen to, and is open to engagement. Assignments can be tough to agree with, and it doesnt always work to plan. I wish there was even more freedom for creativity. Good prof, but perhaps because of how the class is designed, it didnt capture entrepreneurship. Still a good experience.

Simon is such a nice guy, and really cares about his students. It's also clear that he is very passionate about entrepreneurship. The classes are very chill, and he actually makes the course content fun to learn about.

Simon is the best prof I've had at SFU so far. His class is the FIRST business course that actually makes me want to go to class, and the course content in general is very practical, and you learn a lot about businesses. He really wants to help students, makes sure he is available outside of class, and gives back meaningful feedback. Humble too!

Great prof. Simon really cares about his students and knows most by name (all by 4th week). Grading is straightforward, and he always expects you to challenge the assumptions. He is definitely my favourite prof :)
AMAZING PROFESSOR!! His class was fun and he explains everything very well. if you don't mind group projects then this class is a easy A!
